definition: A digital representation of a person powered by AI, often combining voice cloning and video synthesis to create realistic virtual presenters. Used in content creation to maintain consistent delivery.

AI systems that can autonomously take actions, make decisions, and complete multi-step tasks without human intervention at each step. Moves beyond chat-based assistants to agents that browse, code, and execute workflows.
The average cost to acquire one customer through advertising. A key metric for measuring ad campaign efficiency. Lower CPA means more efficient ad spend.
The cost an advertiser pays per 1,000 ad impressions. Standard podcast advertising metric, typically $25-50 for business/tech podcasts.

Legal action taken against AI companies for unauthorized use of copyrighted content in training data or generated outputs. Increasingly common as media companies challenge AI video and image generators.

The ability to maintain a character's appearance, proportions, and visual identity across multiple AI-generated video scenes. Previously the hardest unsolved problem in AI video — tools like Higgsfield Soul Cast now address this with parametric design systems.

The maximum amount of text an AI model can process in a single conversation, measured in tokens. GPT-5.4's 1M token context window means it can analyze roughly 750,000 words at once — equivalent to several full-length books.
Chinese AI lab offering frontier-level AI models at roughly 90% less cost than Western competitors. Disrupted the market by proving high-quality AI doesn't require massive budgets.

The AI architecture behind most image and video generators. Works by learning to remove noise from random static, gradually revealing coherent images. Powers tools like DALL-E, Midjourney, and Runway.

Technology that stitches audio advertisements into podcast episodes in real-time as they're downloaded or streamed, allowing different listeners to hear different ads and enabling ads to be updated after publication.

The process of taking a pre-trained AI model and further training it on specialized data to improve performance for specific tasks. Like teaching a general-purpose assistant to become an expert in your industry.
The most capable and advanced AI models available at any given time. Currently includes GPT-5.x, Claude Opus, and Gemini 3.x. These push the boundaries of what AI can accomplish.
The core AI technology behind chatbots like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. Trained on vast text data to understand and generate human-like language. The foundation of most modern AI tools.

An approach where a single AI system coordinates multiple frontier models (like Claude, GPT, Gemini, Grok) simultaneously, routing each subtask to the best-suited model. Perplexity Computer uses this to complete entire projects from a single prompt.
AI models that can process and generate multiple types of content — text, images, audio, and video — within a single system. The trend driving convergence of AI tools.
The skill of crafting effective instructions for AI models to get optimal outputs. Involves techniques like providing context, examples, and specific formatting requirements.
A technique where AI models pull relevant information from a knowledge base before generating responses. Reduces hallucinations and keeps AI answers grounded in real, current data.

An AI workflow where you provide a script or outline and the tool automatically generates a complete video with scenes, transitions, and visuals. CapCut's rebuilt AI Video Maker brings this to zero cost, cutting production time to 15 minutes.
AI technology that generates video clips from written descriptions. Rapidly evolving space with tools like Runway, Kling, Sora, and Seedance competing for the top spot.
AI technology that replicates a specific person's voice from audio samples, enabling synthetic speech that sounds like the original speaker. Used in podcasting, dubbing, and accessibility.
